chunks.text: It is a data like this and we have to predict that what is the chance of their admission. So this is a regression problem and we would use a neural network to solve this problem. So let's start the video.

So we have already solved two problems. In last video we saw binary classification when we worked on customer churn data set. In last video we did multiclass classification on MNIST data set where there were 10 categories. And today we will work on a regression problem. We will learn that if you have a regression problem then how you can work on it by making an artificial neural network. The data set we will use is a very simple data set where you have some student's data and that data set is about graduate admission. Basically, it is a data set of GRE that how much score your candidates got for GRE, TOEFL, apart from that how much strength they have in their resume. It is a data like this and we have to predict that what is the chance of their admission. So this is a regression problem and we would use a neural network to solve this problem. So let's start the video. So guys, as I told you that in this video we will solve a regression problem with the help of an artificial neural network. And the data set is in front of us. Our data set is a graduate admission data set where we have some columns. This data set is basically for those people who apply for foreign universities. They have to give GRE and TOEFL exam and apart from that they have to make their profile to apply for those universities. So if you notice here, we have 7 columns. So first is GRE score which is max up to 340. TOEFL score is 120. University rating, which university you are from, what is its rating. Your statement of purpose. Also your letter of recommendation strength. So whenever you apply, you have to write a cover letter about yourself that why do you want to pursue that particular course. Also you need a letter of recommendation to apply for these universities. So this recommendation can be of your professors or someone else who has a very strong personality in your field. After that, how much CGPA was there in your undergraduate, whether you have research experience or not. These are all your input columns. And your output column is chance of admit. Whether you will be admitted to that university or not. Between 0 and 1. So you can see this is a regression problem where the output is a number. So we have to work on this data set.
